requirements.description: Research must focus on next-generation AI agents as autonomous systems powered by large AI language models (e.g., large language models or large multimodal models) that can plan, utilise tools, and perform actions autonomously to achieve specified goals based on high-level instructions.

Proposals may address one or more of the following potential research areas: enhancing AI agent autonomy through advanced self-planning and self-optimization; innovation in memory-augmented AI agents for robust long-term reasoning and lifelong learning; developing advanced multi-agent frameworks for collaborative agents including mixed AI architectures; advancing multimodal reasoning capabilities for real-world applications.

Projects must target real-world applications in Apply AI sectors. Portfolio coverage procedure requires that among all funded proposals, at least one each must focus on healthcare, advanced manufacturing, and in-vehicle autonomous driving.
All proposals must incorporate mechanisms for assessing and demonstrating progress, including qualitative and quantitative KPIs, benchmarking, and progress monitoring.
Projects must link to the resources offered by the AI Factories, including the Data Labs.
Proposals must allocate tasks for cohesion activities with the European Partnership on AI, data, and robotics (ADRA) and the CSA HORIZON-CL4-2025-03-HUMAN-18: GenAI4EU central Hub.
Communicable results must be shared with the European R&D community through the AI-on-demand platform.
When possible, proposals should build on and reuse public results from relevant previous funded actions.
Proposals should build on or seek collaboration with relevant projects and develop synergies with other relevant international, European, national, or regional initiatives.
Results should be validated in the Testing and Experiment Facilities where applicable.
